periods.market_context: The period saw the return of Donald Trump as US President, leading to unprecedented tariffs in April and a sharp sell-off in equity markets. This hardline stance on trade shocked markets, causing US dollar, Treasury bonds, and US equities to fall. Erratic news flow exacerbated uncertainty, and the tariff burden may trigger an economic slowdown. Aggressive trade actions damaged the historic safe-haven status of US assets. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East between Israel and Iran caused oil prices to rise from $60 in early May to almost $80 in June, before retreating after US intervention.
periods.thesis: The Sub-Fund is positioned to withstand a broad range of different environments, with an approach to remain patient and lean into risk when rewarded. The manager believes the damage to the US's credibility as a safe-haven could sustain. The outlook suggests President Trump’s America First agenda is reshaping geopolitics and global trade, leading to uncertainty and a growing risk of an economic slowdown. Equities are not cheap, and corporate earnings and valuations may rebase lower if growth falters. The strategy remains conservatively positioned but is poised to act opportunistically if dislocation intensifies, aiming to preserve capital and grow it in real terms over the long term.
periods.decisions_taken:
Equity component:
- Added to existing holdings: Agilent Technologies, Alphabet, Chubb, Diageo.
- Established new holdings: Adobe, L’Oréal, Hubbell, Canadian National Railway, LSEG, LVMH. (Majority of these purchases were made when equity markets had fallen in April).
- Exited positions: Procter & Gamble (P&G) and Moody’s, due to uncertainties about future growth in earnings and risk to current valuations (both were profitable investments).
Gold:
- Modestly reduced the holding.
Bonds/Currencies:
- Reduced the Sub-Fund’s weighted average duration to US Treasuries from five years to two years.
- Trimmed the net US dollar exposure from 25% to 9%.
- Added two Japanese Government Bonds with a maturity of less than two years, based on the yen being cheap relative to sterling and its potential benefit in a risk-off environment.
periods.observed_outcome: The Trojan Fund (Ireland) Class O GBP accumulation shares returned +0.5% in the financial period. During the sharp equity market sell-off in April, the Sub-Fund fell considerably less than the Index, declining -2.7% (peak-to-trough) versus -12.8% for the FTSE All-Share Index (TR), attributed to its defensive asset allocation.

extracted_from: The Sub-Fund is positioned to withstand a broad range of different environments, and our approach is always to remain patient and lean into risk when we are rewarded to do so.
extraction_notes: The document covers the financial period ended 31 July 2025 and provides one snapshot of the manager's thesis and decisions.
